checks.finding: Field-level terms are given concrete, testable shapes rather than prose descriptions: e.g. REQ-001's `conditional_facets[]` if/then reason presence rule (AC-004), the exact digest pattern `^sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$` and semver pattern `^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$` (AC-009/AC-010), the combined RFC-6901 syntax-plus-root-allowlist regex for `dependency_pointers` (AC-017/AC-018), and the `--resolver-version-bump` five-tier enum with an explicit consistency-check rule against the two manifests' own `resolver.version`/`rule_set_revision` (REQ-005, AC-046). Two implementers reading these fields would produce byte-identical schema/comparator behavior. No field or state was found left as an undefined term two implementers could reasonably diverge on.

Checked the potentially interacting rules for internal conflict: REQ-004's 'no axis digest changed → continue, WARN-only (fresh)' branch appears at first read to conflict with REQ-005's minor-tier rule ('run the impact assessment... regardless of whether any context_binding digest also changed'), but AC-045 explicitly resolves the composition by scoping REQ-004's digest-unchanged short-circuit to `none`/`patch` bumps only, and states this as a fix to 'branch-order' rather than leaving it implicit. REQ-005's major-tier-vs-Block precedence is likewise stated explicitly (Goals REQ-005, Edge Cases) rather than left as two silently competing rules. No unresolved direct conflict between requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, or Non-goals was found.

Acceptance-tests.md covers material negative/empty/boundary/failure paths implied by requirements.md's Edge Cases and Goals: empty-array acceptance (AC-003), digest/semver pattern rejection (AC-009/AC-010), all three weakening-verdict states across all three axes including the fail-closed indeterminate case (AC-019/020/023/024/040), zero-digest-change WARN-only branch distinct from metadata-only-refresh (AC-039 vs AC-019), minor/minor-rule-set impact assessment under zero digest change (AC-045), argument-error/exit-3 paths including inconsistent `--resolver-version-bump` declarations (AC-044/046), malformed and out-of-allowlist RFC 6901 pointers (AC-017/018), non-slug-shaped component ids and omitted-components/shared_paths source normalization (AC-015), and duplicate-detection for both `resolved_gates[].id` and `conditional_facets[].facet` (AC-028/AC-047). Coverage is comprehensive relative to the failure modes requirements.md itself names.

requirements.md's Assumptions section lists four assumptions, each either grounded in investigation.md evidence (canonicalizer CLI shape, INV-008; Epic A2 Evidence shape, INV-004; Epic A3 read contract, INV-006) or explicitly flagged as a forward-compatible, non-blocking dependency on future work (Registry/ownership weakening detector) with a stated fallback ('does not depend on this happening to be safe in the meantime'). Each carries an explicit consequence-if-wrong statement (e.g. 'would need a corresponding follow-up revision, out of this spec's own scope to predict'), satisfying resolvability without leaving a hidden, unflagged decision for design/task phase to discover.

REQ-004 defines a governance/irreversible-action boundary in scope for this feature: any axis reporting `weakened`, or a changed axis with an `indeterminate` verdict, must Block unconditionally, 're-approval and re-resolve required, per ADR-0021 item 3' (Goals). This feature does not implement the approval mechanism itself (that is Epic A1's protected sidecar, Non-goals), but it does own, and test, the classification boundary that triggers it: AC-023 (weakened short-circuits to Block even with byte-identical semantic output), AC-024 (indeterminate registry-axis verdict → Block, not the ordinary comparison path), and AC-040 (identical fail-closed rule proven for the ownership axis) all make the Block/no-Block boundary directly testable via `compare-facet-manifest-staleness`'s fixed exit-code contract (0/1/2/3, AC-044).

The spec fixes concrete schema shapes (`required` lists, `additionalProperties: false`, exact patterns/enums for all three new contracts), a fully-enumerated semantic-output field set and comparison algorithm (REQ-004), a closed diagnostic-id table per validator (REQ-006/AC-028), and a normative CLI I/O contract for the staleness comparator (mandatory flags, stdout format `facet-manifest-staleness: <status>:<reason>`, fixed exit codes 0/1/2/3, stderr-only error channel, AC-044) directly in requirements.md/acceptance-tests.md, not deferred as an open product decision. A downstream implementation-policy reviewer has a fixed contract to review against rather than needing to invent field shapes, comparison semantics, or CLI behavior.
